name: Ohio Dietitian License
description: Already licensed as a dietitian? Here's whether your license transfers to Ohio - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.

address.description:
Ohio Board of Dietetics
77 South High Street, 16th Floor
Columbus, OH 43215-6108

requiredDocuments.name: Official Educational Transcripts
requiredDocuments.description: Directly submitted transcripts denoting the awarding of your degree.

requiredDocuments.name: Verification of Registration
requiredDocuments.description: Proof of active status with the Commission on Dietetic Registration (CDR).

requiredDocuments.name: Criminal Records Check
requiredDocuments.description: FBI and Ohio BCI background check reports transmitted directly from the fingerprinting agency to the Board.

fingerprinting.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

mainEntity.name: How much does the Ohio Licensed Dietitian application cost?

acceptedAnswer.text: The initial license costs $228.50 ($225 base fee plus a $3.50 transaction fee levied by the eLicense system).
Unlike many states with specialized dietetics boards, dietitians in Ohio are licensed and regulated by the State Medical Board of Ohio.

mainEntity.name: What continuing education is required in Ohio?

acceptedAnswer.text: You must maintain the CDR's requirement of 75 hours per five-year cycle. Additionally, Ohio dictates that 1 hour of CE during every two-year state renewal cycle must specifically cover ethics, laws, and rules governing practice in Ohio.

mainEntity.name: Does Ohio have reciprocity for dietitians?

acceptedAnswer.text: Ohio has enacted the Dietitian Compact, but compact privileges are not yet issuing. For now, dietitians still apply by endorsement through the state board.

mainEntity.name: How long does it take to transfer my dietitian license to Ohio?
Can I start working in Ohio while my endorsement application is processing?

acceptedAnswer.text: A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.
